tools.name: tm_import_rvt
tools.description:
Import a Revit/BIM model into the Twinmotion visualization pipeline: downloads the source file from a public URL, uploads it to an APS OSS transient bucket, and kicks off an SVF2 + thumbnail translation job. Returns the base64 URN (project_id) used by every other tm_* tool.

When to use: when a user

tools.name: tm_set_environment
tools.description:
Configure the visualization environment (weather, time-of-day, surround context) for a previously imported model. Validates the model exists via APS Model Derivative manifest, then stores the environment config in KV (24h TTL) so tm_render_image and tm_export_video can apply it.

When to use: after

tools.name: tm_render_image
tools.description: Render a still preview image of the model at a specified resolution by pulling the APS Model Derivative thumbnail (capped at 800x800 by the APS endpoint). Also resolves the camera_preset against model metadata to identify which 3D view it maps to, and applies any stored environment config from tm_se

tools.name: tm_export_video
tools.description: Prepare a model for an animated walkthrough / video export by verifying the manifest is complete, then starting a secondary Model Derivative job that produces OBJ geometry (suitable for ingestion into offline rendering pipelines, Blender, or Unreal Engine). Also returns the list of available named v

tools.name: tm_list_scenes
tools.description: Enumerate every 2D/3D view ('scene') baked into the translated model, plus a shallow dump of the model object tree (first 50 top-level nodes across all 3D views), plus the list of completed derivatives (svf2, thumbnail, obj, etc.) available via APS. findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "tm_import_rvt" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Import a Revit/BIM model into the Twinmotion visualization pipeline: downloads the source file from a public URL, uploads it to an APS OSS transient bucket, and…

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "tm_set_environment" appears informational but accepts parameters (environment) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: tm_import_rvt, tm_set_environment, tm_render_image · Private data: tm_import_rvt, tm_set_environment, tm_render_image, tm_export_video, tm_list_scenes · Public sink: tm_import_rvt, tm_set_environment · Destructive: tm_import_rvt, tm_export_video.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: tm_import_rvt, tm_set_environment, tm_render_image · Private data: tm_import_rvt, tm_set_environment, tm_render_image, tm_export_video, tm_list_scenes · Public sink: tm_import_rvt, tm_set_environment · Destructive: tm_import_rvt, tm_export_video

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
